title: cymbopogon nardus var. confertiflorus
intro: Cymbopogon nardus var. confertiflorus, also known as East Indian lemongrass, is a variety of lemongrass, a tropical grass plant native to Southeast Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Cymbopogon nardus var. confertiflorus include reducing stress and anxiety, improving digestion, and relieving pain and inflammation. It is often used to make herbal teas, essential oils, and infusions, which can be prepared by steeping the leaves and stalks in hot water or by steam distillation to extract its active compounds.
